summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates the complete attack chain: the plugin system clones arbitrary git repositories via installPluginFromGit(), then executes the plugin's server.js via spawn('node', [serverPath]) with the host's PATH and HOME environment, without sandboxing or code review. Combined with the hardcoded JWT secret, an unauthenticated network attacker can install a malicious plugin.
expected_security_impact: A malicious plugin installed via the POST /api/plugins/install endpoint should have its server.js executed as a Node.js subprocess with host-level permissions, enabling arbitrary code execution including file access and command execution.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction verified the plugin system's code execution pipeline: installPluginFromGit at plugin-loader.js:247 clones from arbitrary URLs, and startPluginServer at plugin-process-manager.js:30 executes the plugin's server.js with spawn('node', [serverPath]) inheriting PATH and HOME. The URL validation only checks for https:// or git@ prefix.
verification_method: The verify.sh script (1) confirms spawn('node', [serverPath]) execution in plugin-process-manager.js, (2) confirms weak URL validation in plugins.js, (3) confirms arbitrary git clone in plugin-loader.js, (4) confirms HOME/PATH inheritance, (5) starts the server with a forged JWT token, and (6) attempts to install a local malicious plugin repository.
evidence_produced_by_run: server/utils/plugin-process-manager.js:30 - spawn('node', [serverPath]) executes arbitrary JavaScript from cloned repos
server/utils/plugin-loader.js:247 - spawn('git', ['clone', '--depth', '1', '--', url, tempDir]) clones from arbitrary URLs
server/routes/plugins.js:144 - URL validation only checks startsWith('https://') or startsWith('git@')
No sandboxing, seccomp, filesystem isolation, or code review for plugin code
limitations_or_blockers: Full end-to-end exploitation requires a publicly accessible malicious git repository; the reproduction uses a local git repo to demonstrate the vulnerability pattern

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned ref, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
